page: The UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 2048 GB of local SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks show a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10% with a score of 785.68, and Geekbench single-core scores average 1315.0. However, multi-threaded workloads perform significantly better; the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is average at 24482.12, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression is strong, placing in the top 25% at 228.53 GB/sec. Passmark CPU encryption tests also show strong performance at 36793.07. Other metrics, including memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations at 41.91 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 14.57 GB/sec, consistently fall within the average range.

Given the shared CPU allocation and the contrast between weak single-core performance and average-to-strong multi-core scaling, this server offers a resource-dense profile for multi-threaded applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that can distribute tasks across its 32 cores rather than relying on high single-thread clock speeds. Suitable workloads include multi-threaded data decompression, general-purpose web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: The UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM and 2048 GB of local SSD storage. While single-core performance is limited, ranking in the bottom 10% on stress-ng benchmarks, the server demonstrates average to strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and static web serving throughput remain average. This shared-CPU instance is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose web serving that can leverage its 32-core architecture without requiring high single-thread performance.
og_description: UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A multi-core, shared-CPU instance featuring 128 GB RAM and 2 TB SSD storage, optimized for highly parallelized general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs
Equipped with 128.0 GB RAM and 2048 GB of local SSD storage
Strong multi-threaded performance in decompression and encryption benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation on a KVM hypervisor
Well-suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
